LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN NEMEDEZ MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURTS STATES TO ALL COUNSEL PRESENT THAT A REPLY BRIEF OF 259 PAGES WAS JUST FILED ONLINE AS OF THIS MORNING 5-20-19 AND THAT COURTESY COPIES WERE NOT PROVIDED AND THAT THE COURT WAS NOT GIVEN SUFFICIENT TIME TO REVIEW THESE PAPERS. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 7, 2019 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 302 TO GIVE ALL SIDES, AN OPPORTUNITY TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH COURTESY COPIES OF ALL THE PAPERS. UBER MAY FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF UP TO 5 PAGES MAXIMUM TO ADDRESS NEW ARGUMENTS RAISED IN THE REPLY BRIEF BY MAY 31, 2019. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joshua Haffner, Esq. 213-514-5681 of Haffner Law for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Justin Mallory, Esq. 510-540-4881 of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara Specially appearing for Defendant John Nemedez.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

SPEIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT JOHN NARITO NEMEDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION.

Taken Under Submission.*** Defendant submits a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

***After the hearing the Court signs an Order granting Specially appearing Defendant John Narito Nemedez's Motion to quash service of Summons and Complaint for lack of personal jurisdiction. (see scanned order for details)***

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Continued To Jun-07-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael K. Teiman, Esq., Haffner Law PC., Telephone 213-514-5681, appearing for Plaintiff;(CourtCall)
Amy M. Hammond, Esq., Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman, Telephone 925-222-3400, appearing for
Defendant Uber Technologies; (CourtCall)
Heather A. Bourne, Esq., Bremer Whyte, Brown & O'Meara LLP, Telephone 510-540-4881, appearing for Defendant John Nemedez;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court vacates its tentative ruling:

DEFENDANT JOHN NEMEDEZ MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

The Courts states to all counsel present that a reply brief of 259 pages was just filed online as of this morning 5-20-19 and that courtesy copies were not provided and that the Court was not given sufficient time to review these papers. The matter is continued to June 7, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302 to give all sides, an opportunity to provide the Court with courtesy copies of all the papers. Uber may file a supplemental brief up to 5 pages maximum to address new arguments raised in the reply brief by May 31, 2019. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
